title: -Contracts: Future Ford of Concord, Michael Stead Hilltop Ford and Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store for Service, Parts, and Related Equipment for Ford Vehicles

sections.recommendation:
Adopt three Resolutions authorizing the City Manager to:
1. Execute a contract with Future Ford of Concord, to provide service, parts, and
related equipment used on the City’s Ford vehicles, in an amount not to exceed
$690,000, for a period of three years.
2. Execute a contract with Michael Stead Hilltop Ford, to provide service, parts, and
related equipment used on the City’s Ford vehicles, in an amount not to exceed
$690,000, for a period of three years.
3. Execute a contract order with Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store, to
provide service, parts, and related equipment used on the City’s Ford vehicles, in
an amount not to exceed $690,000, for a period of three years.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding is available in FY2026 Budget in Equipment Maintenance (Fund 672). Funding
for future years is subject to Council's approval of the proposed budget and annual
appropriation ordinances.
sections.current_situation:
The Public Works Department requires ongoing access to manufacturer certified
service, parts, and related equipment to ensure the safe and reliable operation of the
City’s Ford vehicles. These contracts will provide original equipment manufacturer
(OEM) parts, software-supported diagnostics, and service functions required for both
routine maintenance and emergency repairs for Ford vehicles. Approximately 52% of
the City’s current on-road fleet consists of Ford manufactured vehicles. This contract will
support a broad range of Ford vehicle types, including, but not limited to the following:
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Ford and Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store for Service, Parts, and February 24, 2026
Related Equipment for Ford Vehicles
 Ford Police Interceptor Utility patrol vehicles
 Ford F150 Lightning electric trucks
 Other Ford light-and heavy-duty trucks, vans, and specialty units built on Ford
on-road chassis
Establishing a three-year contract with Future Ford of Concord, Michael Stead Hilltop
Ford, and Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store will consolidate prior year’s
agreements, ensure continuity of service, and maintain compliance with City
procurement requirements.
The City’s competitive bidding requirements for this purchase have been satisfied by
using Sourcewell Contract No. 080124-FMC. Future Ford of Concord, Michael Stead
Hilltop Ford and Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store are authorized dealers for
Ford-authorized service, parts, and related equipment, and will provide genuine Ford
OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) parts, warranty support, and technical
assistance as needed. Future Ford of Concord, Michael’s Stead Hilltop Ford and
Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store is one of sever
sections.background:
The City Charter allows the City to purchase goods without undergoing a competitive
bid process if the City utilizes pricing obtained by another entity through a competitive
process. On June 13, 2024, Sourcewell released Request for Proposal (RFP) No.
080124 for OEM vehicle parts and supplies. The RFP was available for approximately
50 days, and 15 proposals were submitted. Upon review of proposals, the Sourcewell
Proposal Evaluation Committee selected Ford Motor Company as the most responsive
and responsible proposer to meet the specifications, thereby awarding Contract No.
080124-FMC.
On January 2, 2025, Sourcewell executed a contract with Ford Motor Company,
authorizing participating agencies to purchase equipment, parts, and related services
Page 3 of 7
Multi-Year Contract with Future Ford of Concord, Michael Stead Hilltop CONSENT CALENDAR
Ford and Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store for Service, Parts, and February 24, 2026
Related Equipment for Ford Vehicles
through authorized regional Ford dealers, including Future Ford of Concord, Michael
Stead Hilltop Ford, and Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store. Article 3, Section 1
of the referenced contract states that participating entities may issue purchase orders
directly to the supplier’s authorized regional dealers using pre-negotiated pricing and
terms of the Sourcewell contract.
Throughout the year, Public Works purchases parts via cooperative contracts to ensure
that City departments receive the best pricing. These Ford parts are specialized and
require OEM authorization to ensure compatibility, quality, warranty compliance, and
access to manufacturer-supported diagnostic functions.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The City prioritizes maintaining its vehicles in the most efficient and environmentally
responsible condition possible.
Timely preventive maintenance supported by using OEM parts helps extend vehicle life,
minimize breakdowns, and maintain compliance with emissions standar
sections.rationale:
Purchasing OEM parts from Future Ford of Concord, Michael Stead Hilltop Ford, and
Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store is the most cost-effective method of
maintaining the City’s Ford vehicles. The use of genuine OEM parts ensures proper fit,
performance, and warranty compliance, reducing vehicle downtime and extending the
service life of existing equipment. Maintaining an adequate supply of OEM parts also
ensures that Public Works can complete preventive maintenance and repair work in a
timely manner, minimizing the risk of service interruptions to City operations.
Utilizing pricing obtained through Sourcewell Contract No. 080124-FMC satisfies the
City’s competitive bidding requirements and ensures the most competitive pricing
available.
sections.alternatives:
Public Works could procure non-OEM or aftermarket parts. However, aftermarket parts
may not meet manufacturer specifications and could void existing warranties or reduce
vehicle performance and reliability. Utilizing genuine OEM parts through Future Ford of
Concord, Michael Stead Hilltop Ford, and Nicholas K Corporation dba The Ford Store
ensures proper fit, quality, and continued warranty protection.

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution: Future Ford of Concord

Page 5 of 7
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: FUTURE FORD OF CONCORD FOR SERVICE, PARTS, AND RELATED
EQUIPMENT FOR FORD VEHICLES
WHEREAS, original equipment manufacturer (OEM) service, parts, and related
equipment are needed by the City; and
WHEREAS, on June 13, 2024, Sourcewell released Request for Proposal No. 080124 for
OEM vehicle parts and supplies; the solicitation remained open approximately fifty days,
and fifteen proposals were submitted; and
WHEREAS, upon review of proposals, the Sourcewell Proposal Evaluation Committee
selected Ford Motor Company as the most responsive proposer to meet the
specifications, thusly awarding Contract No. 080124-FMC; and
WHEREAS, Future Ford of Concord is an authorized regional dealer for Ford Motor
Company and will provide genuine OEM parts, warranty support, and technical
assistance to the City under the pricing and terms of Sourcewell Contract No. 080124-
FMC; and
WHEREAS, service labor is restricted by Ford to OEM authorized dealerships and cannot
be competitively bid among independent vendors; therefore, a waiver of solicitation for
service labor is authorized; and
WHEREAS, for all Sourcewell contracts, the administrative fee is paid by the supplier
directly to Sourcewell and is not passed on to the City; and
WHEREAS, funds for this purchase are available in the Public Works Department
Equipment Maintenance Fund (672) for FY 2026,and spending in future years is subject
to Council's approval of the proposed citywide budget and annual appropriation
ordinances.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract with Future Ford of Concord for service,
maintenance parts, and related equipment for Ford Motor Company in an amount not to
exceed $690,000 for a three-year period ending November 14, 2028.
Page 6 of 7
RE
